title: On the Alignment between Fairness and Accuracy from the Perspective of Adversarial Robustness
abstract: While numerous work has been proposed to address fairness in machine learning, existing methods do not guarantee fair predictions under imperceptible feature perturbation, and a seemingly fair model can suffer from large group-wise disparities under such perturbation. Moreover, while adversarial training has been shown to be reliable in improving a model’s robustness to defend against adversarial feature perturbation that deteriorates accuracy, it has not been properly studied in the context of adversarial perturbation against fairness. To tackle these challenges, in this paper, we study the problem of adversarial attack and adversarial robustness w.r.t. two terms: fairness and accuracy. From the adversarial attack perspective, we propose a unified structure for adversarial attacks against fairness which brings together common notions in group fairness, and we theoretically prove the equivalence of adversarial attacks against different fairness notions. Further, we derive the connections between adversarial attacks against fairness and those against accuracy. From the adversarial robustness perspective, we theoretically align robustness to adversarial attacks against fairness and accuracy, where robustness w.r.t. one term enhances robustness w.r.t. the other term. Our study suggests a novel way to unify adversarial training w.r.t. fairness and accuracy, and experiments show our proposed method achieves better robustness w.r.t. both terms.
impact: his paper aims to bridge two perspectives on adversarial robustness: robustness concerning fairness notions and robustness concerning accuracy. We believe this work will benefit the trustworthy machine learning community, par- A potential consequence of our method is the generation of fairness adversarial samples, which, if not properly supervised, could be maliciously exploited to compromise trustworthiness. This potential misuse calls for proactive defenses and rigorous oversight to prevent adversarial manipulations from undermining fairness-aware AI systems.

query_input.scenario: Adversarial fairness attack via node injections in social network GNNs
query_input.issue: Injected nodes manipulate graph structure to skew fairness-sensitive predictions against specific groups.
hazard: Adversarial perturbations crafted to compromise fairness cause disproportionate misclassification across demographic groups, undermining group fairness.
exposure: Individuals whose social network data is used for credit scoring or recommendations

hipporag_result.vulnerability: GNN-based credit scoring models lack robustness to adversarial node injections, rendering fairness protections ineffective for marginalized groups.
hipporag_result.impact: Targeted demographic groups receive systematically unfavorable credit decisions, deepening financial exclusion and inequality.
hipporag_result.dose_response: Adversarial perturbations that undermine group fairness, through credit scoring using social network data, trigger GNN models' vulnerability to node injections, causing disproportionately unfavorable credit decisions for marginalized groups and deepening financial exclusion.

dose_response: Adversarial node injections distort GNN embeddings by overwhelming minority group nodes with few connections, leading to biased credit scoring and widened economic disparities.
vulnerability: Minority group nodes with few connections are easily overwhelmed by adversarial injections that distort GNN embeddings.
impact: Biased credit scoring and predatory recommendations widen economic disparities for protected social groups.
